summarization.a: The graph shows the relationship between SF<sub>6</sub> plasma exposure time and EPC (etch per cycle) in Ångströms.
summarization.b: The graph illustrates the effect of plasma pump time on EPC (etch per cycle) in Ångströms.
summarization.c: The graph depicts the impact of TMA dosing time on EPC (etch per cycle) in Ångströms.
summarization.d: The graph shows the influence of TMA pump time on EPC (etch per cycle) in Ångströms.

d.question: Based on the shown times, what would be the best ALE process in terms of duration and self-limiting behaviour?

c.question: What happens at a dose time of 0.1 s of TMA?
c.answer: c	At this dose time there is a negative EPC indicating that there is deposition of aluminum.

a.question: What is the saturated dose time of the plasma step?
a.answer: a	The saturated time is already around 5s, however the process soft-saturates.

b.question: Are pump times of 5 s sufficient for all the purging during the ALE cycle?
b.answer: Both subfigure b and d show that 5 s is sufficient purge time.
